content.text: The following chart compares quarterly total returns for the Growth and Balanced mandates against their respective benchmarks. Both mandates outperformed across all four quarters of the period.

content.title: Quarterly Total Return (%)
content.caption: Fig. 1 — Quarterly total returns, Growth vs Balanced, Q1–Q4 2026. Benchmark is composite blended index.

content.text: Monthly NAV progression (rebased to 100 at 1 January 2026) illustrates the compound growth trajectory of each mandate. The Growth mandate reached 122.4 by year end, reflecting strong equity market conditions.

content.title: NAV Progression — Rebased to 100 (Jan 2026)
content.caption: Fig. 2 — Monthly NAV, rebased to 100 at 1 Jan 2026. Growth (navy), Balanced (blue), Income (amber).

content.text: The strategic asset allocation reflects a balanced approach to long-term risk-adjusted return. Global equities represent the largest single allocation at 45%, consistent with the mandate's growth orientation.

content.title: Strategic Asset Allocation
content.caption: Fig. 3 — Strategic asset allocation as at 30 June 2026.

content.text: The bar chart below decomposes total portfolio risk (ex-ante annualised volatility) by asset class contribution. Fixed income and alternatives provide the strongest diversification benefit relative to their weight.

content.title: Risk Contribution by Asset Class (%)
content.caption: Fig. 4 — Ex-ante annualised volatility contribution by asset class.

content.text: All figures as at 30 June 2026. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. This document is for professional investors only.
